title: Indus Waters Treaty fallout? Nearly a third of Pakistan faces water crisis
description: Pakistan grapples with a severe water shortage affecting one-third of its population. Last year, India suspended the Indus Waters Treaty after terror attacks. This has intensified water scarcity in Sindh and Balochistan. Irrigation canals face significant deficits. Allegations of unequal water distribution fuel political disputes. Farmers struggle as cultivation is hampered. The crisis highlights vulnerabilities in Pakistan's water management.

With the species' main hatching season in June and July, concerns are rising about its impact on native wildlife, farms, and ecosystems as populations expand beyond Florida.

description: A joint police team, including central forces, conducted an overnight search at Trinamool leader Abhishek Banerjee's Kolkata residence. The operation, linked to a Paschim Medinipur case, involved breaking locks after no response from occupants. Chief Minister Mamata Banerjee rushed to the scene amidst the ensuing political controversy.

From Lebanon ceasefire to nuclear talks - what Iran proposed in 'Islamabad Agreement'
description: Iran has outlined conditions for a potential US agreement, prioritizing sanctions relief and maritime access over immediate nuclear program discussions. Tehran insists on lifting the US naval blockade and a new arrangement for the Strait of Hormuz, while also seeking to include Lebanon in the deal.

title: Watch: US 'executes' Venezuela's 'bloodthirsty' prison gang leader Niño Guerrero in strike
description: US President Donald Trump announced a US military strike, conducted at his direction, killed Hector Rusthenford Guerrero Flores, known as Niño Guerrero, leader of the Venezuelan gang Tren de Aragua. Trump stated the operation was coordinated with Venezuelan authorities, linking it to his anti-crime and immigration agenda.  The gang is designated a foreign terrorist organization by Washington.

One year after Air India Ahmedabad crash, no report released
description: The London-bound AI171 crashed immediately after taking off from Ahmedabad airport on June 12, 2025. Of the 242 people on board, 241 were killed, and 19 other victims were from the ground at the hostel complex of Ahmedabad’s B J Medical College that the plane crashed into. The closest international parallel to AI171 may be Ethiopian Airlines Flight 302, the 2019 Boeing 737 MAX crash that killed 157 people.

Delhiites to pay more for power from next month
description: Delhi power bills are set to increase for lakhs of consumers as the DERC allows discoms to recover higher fuel and power purchase costs. Consumers in BYPL areas will see a 5.7% rise, while BRPL consumers face a 3.4% increase, effective from July. This hike follows a sharp rise in procurement costs due to increased demand and expensive market purchases.

Govt-gifted bull to replace farmer’s wife yoked to the plough turns out to be unfit
description: A distressed landless farmer from Latur, who was forced to yoke his wife to a plough after losing a bull, received a replacement animal that was found unfit for work. Chief Minister Devendra Fadnavis has now directed officials to take the farmer to a cattle market to personally select a healthy bull, following criticism of the initial replacement.

description: A Delhi court deferred hearing on Yash Yadav's interim bail plea for the NEET UG exam, deeming it premature as he lacks an admit card. The court will revisit the matter on June 16, after seeking a response from the CBI regarding Yadav's request for bail to attend his sister's wedding and the examination.

title: Bihar residents waited 12 years for Rs 26-crore overbridge. It shuts within week due to damage
description: Traffic on the Buxar–Baruna rail overbridge has been suspended after a slab on a pillar was damaged, exacerbating the woes of residents. With the bridge and the adjacent railway crossing now closed, commuters face severe inconvenience and long delays, as authorities scramble for temporary solutions.
